metrics.narrative: DuBois manages $300-400M in combined direct and indirect spend across a 9-10 person sourcing team (Avoma Intro Call, 3 Dec 2025), and Tharun has repeatedly said he needs facts to justify the AI/CLM spend to upper management: "I need to push it through the upper management. I need to visually see how it interacts and how it works" (Avoma AI Discussion, 26 Feb 2026). No dollar-quantified savings, cycle-time, or error-rate figure has ever been tied to that spend base despite Tharun asking Zycus for a ballpark and pricing multiple times.

champion.narrative: Tharunkumar Chandirasekaran (Sourcing Analyst, self-described "super user") has attended all 7 calls, driven scope refinement, coordinated legal-team involvement, and proactively reassured Zycus during the pause that "it's not like we are pursuing this with a different supplier" (Avoma Tentative Connect, 26 May 2026). What has developed: sustained multi-call engagement and internal coordination of legal and IT stakeholders. What is missing: he is a working-level analyst who defers budget and exec decisions upward, and has not looped Jackie Zhang or the C-suite back into any recorded conversation since the intro window, so his advocacy has not yet converted into executive sponsorship.

competition.narrative: Coupa is deeply embedded (invoicing 3-way match, supplier onboarding, indirect P2P) and DuBois has said it does not plan to replace it ("I don't believe we'll be getting rid of Coupa", Avoma AI Discussion, 26 Feb 2026), positioning Zycus as a complementary CLM/sourcing/AI layer rather than a rip-and-replace. SAP Ariba is used indirectly through third-party Blackstone for reverse auctions and is a minor factor. DuBois described an approximately 10-vendor bake-off intended to shortlist to a top 3 by end of Q1 2026 (missed due to the pause), so Zycus remains one of several vendors still in play rather than a confirmed front-runner.

identify_pain.narrative: Tharun described a rudimentary, under-leveraged contract repository ("apart from that, that repository doesn't serve any good", Avoma Discovery Session, 12 Dec 2025), manual trade-agreement tracking in D365 with no renewal alerts, and fragmented indirect-spend supplier lists stemming from 20-30 acquisitions over 20 years ("the information is a lot fragmented... we don't have any approved vendor list", Avoma Discovery Session, 12 Dec 2025). The pain owner is Tharun's sourcing team, and the pain has been consistently reaffirmed across the 14 Jan, 29 Jan, and 26 Feb calls in the form of specific product questions (obligation tracking, version control, risk-flagging on redlines).

paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal-redline, or procurement-channel discussion has occurred in any of the 7 calls; the evaluation has not progressed past feature demos and legal-team fit assessment. Chris Klein and Laura are engaged as future CLM users evaluating the tool, not as a paper-process gate yet. Visibility would come from asking DuBois what its internal procurement/legal sign-off chain looks like once the Q3 restart begins.

economic_buyer.narrative: Jackie Zhang (VP Procurement) is the Salesforce Decision Maker / budget-authority contact and was referenced as having met with Grace Kim before the 12 Dec 2025 discovery call ("you and I met with Jackie last week", Avoma Discovery Session, 12 Dec 2025), but she has not appeared on any of the 7 recorded calls since. Tharun defers exec and budget sign-off to her and DuBois's C-suite, and a scheduling ask for her calendar on 11 Mar 2026 was never confirmed as completed. Her absence for roughly 7 months is a real gap in direct budget-authority engagement.

decision_process.narrative: The process ran: intro (3 Dec 2025) -> discovery (12 Dec 2025) -> two CLM/sourcing demos (14 & 29 Jan 2026) -> AI deep-dive (26 Feb 2026) -> CLM deep dive with legal (11 Mar 2026), with a stated plan to shortlist top 3 of roughly 10 vendors by end of Q1 2026 and then run group demos with Jackie, Ryan Anderson and legal (Avoma Demo Part 2, 29 Jan 2026). That shortlist milestone was missed: on 26 May 2026 Tharun said DuBois introduced a new internal IT-governance team with a cross-department approval workflow that now gates any vendor follow-up, and the project itself was paused to a Q3 restart. The process is currently stalled inside DuBois's own internal review, not moving through the originally planned steps.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented across calls: contract lifecycle management is the top-stated priority (clause libraries, risk profiling, automated redlining, dual supplier/customer contract support - Avoma Intro Call, 3 Dec 2025), followed by sourcing/RFP tooling for a newly-added indirect-spend responsibility, and an AI/agentic layer (Merlin, autonomous negotiation) evaluated in depth on the 26 Feb 2026 AI Discussion call. Mandatory bidirectional integration with D365 and Coupa, with no plan to replace either, is a hard requirement stated on the 3 Dec 2025 intro call. Legal team members Chris Klein and Laura were added as co-equal evaluators for CLM feature-fit and ease of use ("New legal team holds equal weight with sourcing in CLM vendor selection", Avoma AI Discussion, 26 Feb 2026).

No other activity accompanied this change, which suggests the deal is stalling rather than progressing, and the team should be asking what's actually blocking it from closing.

hard.ais_why:
• Champion Tharun Chandirasekaran engaged the January 29 2026 demo on agentic capability across multiple dimensions. He pulled for custom AI prompt creation via the Merlin Agentic platform for end-to-end negotiation flows, asked for the rationale behind AI-suggested discount percentages, and requested a backend chemical supplier search via the autonomous agent. AI importance is rated Very Critical.
• Tharun raised concrete agentic-platform questions: surfacing Anna's negotiation rationale in the UI, Outlook integration for auto-syncing contract red-lines, RFX Scoring Pro automating supplier response scoring with AI, and AI-driven supplier identification via supplier.io. These are capability-fit probes, not generic curiosity. The discovery has moved past pitch into evaluation.
• Five completed meetings between December 2025 and March 2026 with Tharun as the consistent champion, plus legal counsel Chris Klein joining for contract module review and Ryan Anderson handling integration. Pricing has been quoted across iContract, iSource, iAnalyze, iRisk and ANA modules. With Very Critical AI posture and an agentic AI champion driving the relationship, this is a high-confidence Hungry deal.

deltas.subject: No quantified ROI or business case.
